If you are working with a Payment Service Provider who can give you access to Apple Pay without an Apple Developer Account, the process outlined below will be different and you should contact the the Payment Service Provider for additional help on configuring your Merchant Assets.
Important : Do not lose this Payment Processing Identity, because it will be used by your Payment Processor.

The benefit of this workflow is you would not need to export your Payment Processing Identity from your Keychain to provide it to the Payment Processor, they would already have the required private key from the CSR that they had previously generated.
The Keychain on macOS does this for you and so you do not have to worry about it, but if using an external tool, you will need to do this by hand.
Note, if you plan to process payments for , and you will actually be using , make sure to include the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) in the registration process.
If all goes well, your domain should be verified and show up as “Verified” in the Merchant ID Configuration page.
Servers negotiating TLS connections for Apple Pay on the Web need to use one of the supported cipher suites described in .
Make sure that you are using a valid TLS certificate.
For example, make sure that this certificate is setup with a proper chain of trust and also adheres to for Safari.
Make sure the leaf certificate being used on your domain follows a correct chain of trust through the intermediate certificates to the root certificate.
Capturing video in Apple ProRes requires manual configuration of the ’s .
For this reason, set the of your to , which specifies that the should not automatically configure its inputs and outputs.
As described in , Apple ProRes 422 codecs require 10-bits-per-channel video sources, and Apple ProRes 4444 codecs support up to 12-bits-per-channel and up to 16 bits for the alpha channel.

If you require a greater amount of control when writing the movie file, or you need access to video samples before they are written to a movie file, then you can use an to get access to the video samples.
For this reason, you should check for support of Apple ProRes 422 codecs only after you have configured the capture device to capture in an ‘x422’ format.
Use the method to set a recording delegate and start recording to a file.
When you create the , specify outputSettings that use your preferred Apple ProRes 422 video codec.
You can request recommended settings from the : Take the video samples that you receive in the delegate method and append them to your movie file : When you decide to stop recording, call .
Status bar content elements must be readable or visible to the user.
Your app’s status bar style or should be set to a value that’s compatible with your app’s background color: or .
For example, to set a light content status bar, use the following:

For example, to set the status bar’s content intended for use on dark backgrounds: If a particular view controller is embedded as a child or resides inside a stack, its parent or navigation controller determines the status bar style.

For example: For every defined in your storyboard that wants to give status bar style control over to its children, be sure to set its Custom Class name to match the subclass name on the Identity inspector.
If you return or do not override this method, the status bar style for is used.
For a view controller that scrolls its content, use it to apply both and to the .
Starting in iOS 26, reduce your use of custom backgrounds in navigation elements and controls.
While the techniques in this document remain valid for iOS 18 and earlier, prefer to remove custom effects and let the system determine the navigation bar background appearance.
Any custom backgrounds and appearances you use in the navigation bar might overlay or interfere with Liquid Glass or other effects that the system provides, such as the scroll edge effect.
To standardize the navigation bar’s appearance between these versions of iOS, use the API.
Use the following example to apply an opaque navigation bar colored with white title text.
In the Attributes Inspector pane turn on these Appearances: “Standard”, “Compact”, “Scroll Edge”, and “Compact Scroll Edge”.

If you use a third-party library to create, verify, and sign the JWT, make sure the library meets the following criteria for Sign in with Apple: Supports digital signatures Supports the required headers and payload claims Does not decode using byte format
If your app links against static libraries, confirm that they all use a build phase to export their header files.
The produced app archive contains header files when static libraries use a build phase to export these files.
